Name: traveling-salesman
Description: Traveling Salesman research domain at city/neighborhood/address scale. The rulebook normalizes finite weighted city graphs, validates explicit ordered-tour witnesses, records typed frontier obligations, derives a degree-two lower bound, and certifies the supplied Gridville route as optimal for its declared finite instance without claiming a general solver.

__meta__.Description: Rulebook metadata and explicit research-program guardrails.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the metadata row.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: Declared type of the metadata value.

Cities.Description: Cities provide the outer spatial boundary for TSP instances.

schema.name: CountryCode
schema.Description: ISO-style country code for the city.

schema.name: CoordinateSystem
schema.Description: Coordinate convention used by addresses in this city.

data.Description: Deterministic five-address fixture used to bring the TSP Postgres substrate online.

Neighborhoods.Description: Neighborhoods partition a city into first-class spatial clusters.

schema.name: CityName
schema.Description: Display name of the containing city.

schema.name: ClusterKind
schema.Description: Semantic role of the neighborhood in the initial fixture.

schema.name: IsQuotientNode
schema.Description: Whether the neighborhood is currently used as a quotient-graph node.

schema.name: RequiredBoundaryDegree
schema.Description: Required external incidence in the quotient cycle.

schema.name: QuotientScopeId
schema.Description: Instance scope without introducing a relationship cycle.

Addresses.Description: Addresses are visitable physical locations. Coordinates support visualization; edge costs remain explicit graph data.

schema.name: NeighborhoodName
schema.Description: Display name of the containing neighborhood.

schema.name: City
schema.Description: City reached through the address's neighborhood.

schema.name: XCoordinate
schema.Description: Local X coordinate for visualization and later geometric experiments.

schema.name: YCoordinate
schema.Description: Local Y coordinate for visualization and later geometric experiments.

schema.name: IsDepotCandidate
schema.Description: Whether this address may serve as a depot.

TSPInstances.Description: One finite Traveling Salesman problem instance over required city addresses.

schema.name: DepotAddress
schema.Description: Address fixed as the tour depot.

schema.name: IsSymmetric
schema.Description: Whether each canonical edge cost applies in both directions.

schema.name: SearchPolicy
schema.Description: Research policy governing when search is allowed.

schema.name: CountOfTravelEdges
schema.Description: Number of canonical undirected travel edges.

schema.name: CountOfInadmissibleEdges
schema.Description: Edge rows failing canonical pair, endpoint, availability, or instance checks.

schema.name: CountOfNonUniqueEdgePairRows
schema.Description: Rows participating in a duplicated canonical unordered edge pair.

schema.name: ExpectedUndirectedEdgeCount
schema.Description: n(n-1)/2 expected edges for a complete undirected graph.

schema.name: IsCompleteUndirectedGraph
schema.Description: Whether the instance has the expected edge count, no inadmissible rows, and no duplicated unordered pair rows.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Scope and caveats for the instance.

data.Notes: A five-stop complete weighted graph. It calibrates representation and tour validation; it does not establish a general TSP algorithm.
Three edge rows equal n(n-1)/2, but AB appears twice and BC is missing; count alone must not certify completeness.
Two cheap internal triangles and expensive crossings; local degree-two bound is sound but disconnected and non-tight.
Only the five ring edges and one B-D chord are available; degree-two forcing selects the ring without branching.

TSPGraphInvariantChecks.Description: Graph-normalization acceptance checks, including a count-preserving duplicate-pair counterexample.

schema.name: ExpectedNonUniqueEdgePairRows
schema.Description: Expected number of rows participating in duplicated pair keys.

schema.name: IsPassing
schema.Description: Whether all graph expectations agree with derived values.

data.DisplayName: Gridville five-stop graph has one edge per unordered pair
Expected edge count cannot hide a duplicated pair and omitted pair

InstanceStops.Description: Junction entity selecting addresses into a TSP instance without introducing a many-to-many relationship.

TravelEdges.Description: Canonical undirected weighted edges between instance stops. The edge entity resolves the address-to-address many-to-many relation.

schema.name: TravelCost

schema.name: CanonicalPairKey

schema.name: IsCanonicalPairKeyCorrect

schema.name: IsCanonicalEndpointOrder

schema.name: PairMultiplicity

schema.name: IsSelfLoop
schema.Description: Whether both endpoints are the same stop.

schema.name: IsWithinInstance
schema.Description: Whether the edge and both endpoints belong to one instance.

schema.name: IsAdmissible

CandidateTours.Description: Explicit candidate tours. Validity is derived; optimality is deliberately not inferred.

schema.name: RequiredStopCount

schema.name: CountOfNonUniqueVisits

schema.name: CountOfNonUniqueSequences

schema.name: CountOfNonRequiredStops
schema.Description: Tour stops not marked required by the instance.

schema.name: CountOfDepotStops
schema.Description: Number of depot visits in the ordered stop list.

schema.name: CountOfDepotPositionViolations
schema.Description: Depot rows not placed at sequence position one.

schema.name: CountOfInvalidLegs
schema.Description: Tour legs failing membership, sequence, edge, or instance checks.

schema.name: CountOfCycleDegreeViolations

schema.name: CountOfNonUniqueLegOrders

schema.name: CountOfPassingOptimalityCertificates

schema.name: ResidualClaim

data.DisplayName: Counterexample with duplicated A-B and missing E-A

TourStops.Description: Ordered candidate-tour visits. Multiplicity and sequence uniqueness are derived from the row set.

schema.name: FromTourCandidate
schema.Description: Candidate tour owning the from visit.

schema.name: ToTourCandidate
schema.Description: Candidate tour owning the to visit.

schema.name: FromInstanceStop
schema.Description: Instance stop at the start of the leg.

schema.name: ToInstanceStop

schema.name: FromSequencePosition

schema.name: ToSequencePosition

schema.name: EdgeTSPInstance

schema.name: EdgeTravelCost
schema.Description: Optimization cost of the selected edge.

schema.name: EdgeIsAdmissible

schema.name: LegOrderMultiplicity

schema.name: IsInTourInstance

schema.name: TravelCost

LocalDegreeBounds.Description: One degree-two lower-bound witness per required stop. Each row names two incident edges and proves all other incident edges are no cheaper than the second selected edge.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the local bound.

schema.name: TSPInstance
schema.Description: Instance whose tour degree requirement is being bounded.

schema.name: InstanceStop
schema.Description: Required stop receiving the local degree-two bound.

schema.name: StopAdmissibleIncidentEdgeCount
schema.Description: Current admissible incident-edge count at the stop.

schema.name: FirstEdgeFromStop
schema.Description: First selected edge's canonical first endpoint.

schema.name: FirstEdgeToStop
schema.Description: First selected edge's canonical second endpoint.

schema.name: SecondEdgeFromStop
schema.Description: Second selected edge's canonical first endpoint.

schema.name: SecondEdgeToStop
schema.Description: Second selected edge's canonical second endpoint.

schema.name: FirstEdgeCost
schema.Description: Cost of the first selected edge.

schema.name: SecondEdgeCost
schema.Description: Cost of the second selected edge.

schema.name: IsFirstEdgeIncident
schema.Description: Whether the first selected edge is incident to the bounded stop.

schema.name: IsSecondEdgeIncident
schema.Description: Whether the second selected edge is incident to the bounded stop.

schema.name: AreSelectedEdgesDistinct
schema.Description: Whether the two selected edge rows are distinct.

schema.name: AreSelectedCostsOrdered
schema.Description: Whether the first selected edge is no more expensive than the second.

schema.name: RequiredDominanceCheckCount
schema.Description: Number of other incident edges that must be compared against the second selected cost.

schema.name: CountOfDominanceChecks
schema.Description: Represented comparisons against other incident edges.

schema.name: CountOfFailedDominanceChecks
schema.Description: Dominance comparisons that fail incidence, distinctness, uniqueness, or cost ordering.

schema.name: IsTwoCheapestWitness
schema.Description: Whether this row and its child comparisons certify the two cheapest admissible incident edges.

schema.name: LocalBoundCost
schema.Description: Certified sum of the two cheapest incident edge costs; zero when the witness is invalid.

IncidentDominanceChecks.Description: Witness rows proving every unselected incident edge is at least as expensive as the second edge named by a LocalDegreeBound.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the dominance check.

schema.name: OtherEdge
schema.Description: Unselected incident edge compared against the second selected edge.

schema.name: BoundStop
schema.Description: Stop bounded by the parent witness.

schema.name: FirstSelectedEdge
schema.Description: First edge selected by the parent witness.

schema.name: SecondSelectedEdge
schema.Description: Second edge selected by the parent witness.

schema.name: SecondSelectedEdgeCost
schema.Description: Cost of the second selected edge.

schema.name: OtherEdgeCost
schema.Description: Cost of the unselected incident edge.

schema.name: OtherEdgeMultiplicity
schema.Description: Number of comparison rows in the same local witness naming this other edge.

schema.name: IsOtherEdgeIncident
schema.Description: Whether the compared edge is incident to the bounded stop.

schema.name: IsOtherEdgeUnselected
schema.Description: Whether the compared edge differs from both selected edges.

schema.name: IsOtherEdgeUnique
schema.Description: Whether this unselected edge appears exactly once among the parent's comparisons.

schema.name: IsDominatedBySecondCost
schema.Description: Whether the unselected edge is no cheaper than the second selected edge.

schema.name: IsPassing
schema.Description: Whether the comparison is incident, unselected, unique, and cost-dominated.

InstanceLowerBounds.Description: Global lower bounds obtained by summing certified local degree-two bounds and dividing by two because each tour edge is incident to two stops.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the lower bound.

schema.name: RequiredStopCount
schema.Description: Required stop count of the instance.

schema.name: CountOfLocalDegreeBounds
schema.Description: Local degree-bound rows represented for the instance.

schema.name: CountOfInvalidLocalDegreeBounds
schema.Description: Local degree-bound rows that do not carry a valid two-cheapest witness.

schema.name: TotalLocalDegreeBoundCost
schema.Description: Sum of the local two-edge lower bounds across all required stops.

schema.name: LowerBoundCost
schema.Description: Certified global tour lower bound after double-count correction.

schema.name: IsCertified
schema.Description: Whether every required stop has one valid local witness and the global double-counting aggregation is closed.

schema.name: CountOfCertifiedSupplementalTerms
schema.Description: Certified terms contributing to the adjustment.

schema.name: SupplementalBoundAdjustment
schema.Description: Signed sum of certified repair terms.

schema.name: IsSupplementalBoundCertified
schema.Description: Whether the required repair terms are all certified.

TSPBoundTerms.Description: Signed additive and constraint terms composing reusable lower-bound certificates.

schema.name: TermKind
schema.Description: Base, insertion, release, parity, or summary term.

schema.name: CountsTowardAdjustment
schema.Description: Whether TermValue contributes to the supplemental adjustment.

schema.name: IsCertified
schema.Description: Whether the term has a represented witness.

data.Justification: A cycle crosses every cut evenly; a Hamiltonian cycle connecting both selected components must cross positively, hence at least twice.
The witnessed local two-cheapest incidence bound is six.
Cut parity requires two crossings and every crossing edge costs ten.
Opening each three-cycle to accept two crossings releases one cost-one internal edge per component.

OptimalityCertificates.Description: Finite-instance optimality certificates. Passing requires a valid Hamiltonian witness, a certified lower bound for the same instance, and equality between candidate cost and lower bound.

schema.name: CandidateTour
schema.Description: Candidate whose optimality is being certified.

schema.name: InstanceLowerBound
schema.Description: Certified lower bound compared with the candidate cost.

schema.name: LowerBoundTSPInstance
schema.Description: Instance owned by the lower-bound certificate.

schema.name: CandidateTravelCost
schema.Description: Derived travel cost of the candidate.

schema.name: CandidateIsHamiltonianCycleWitness
schema.Description: Whether the candidate is a valid Hamiltonian-cycle witness.

schema.name: LowerBoundIsCertified
schema.Description: Whether the lower-bound construction is certified.

schema.name: IsSameInstance
schema.Description: Whether candidate and lower bound refer to the same TSP instance.

schema.name: IsBoundTight
schema.Description: Whether the candidate cost equals the certified lower bound.

schema.name: IsPassing
schema.Description: Whether bound equality certifies this candidate as optimal for the declared finite instance.

TSPExecutionRuns.Description: Execution attempts against a concrete substrate; blocked and failed runs remain first-class evidence.

pinned archive; build; db; test; show; standalone ledger finalizer
data.FailureReason: All generated-substrate and peer checks passed. The ledger finalizer rejected PostgreSQL NUMERIC text 14.0000000000000000 against the presentation string 14 before canonical closure was persisted.

TSPEdgeStates.Description: Epistemically typed state of one edge in one inference snapshot.

schema.name: DecisionStatus
schema.Description: UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, or SUPERSEDED.

schema.name: EpistemicStatus
schema.Description: Why that decision status is justified.

schema.name: IsDecided
schema.Description: Whether the edge is no longer unknown.

schema.name: CommitmentRank
schema.Description: Ordered strength in the edge commitment lattice.

schema.name: CommitmentPolarity
schema.Description: Positive inclusion, zero unknown, or negative exclusion.

schema.name: NecessityScope
schema.Description: Scope in which the commitment holds.

schema.name: IsTerminalCommitment
schema.Description: Whether deterministic closure no longer treats the edge as open.

TSPClusterBoundaryStates.Description: Finite undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states for a neighborhood cluster.

schema.name: Neighborhood

TSPInferenceRules.Description: Declared structural inference contracts, including soundness, completeness, runtime, memory, applicability, and certificate shape.

schema.Description: Declared asymptotic runtime for the represented step.

schema.name: MemoryClass
schema.Description: Declared asymptotic memory for the represented step.

schema.name: CertificateType
schema.Description: Witness or certificate emitted by the rule.

data.Applicability: Every address has exactly one neighborhood and every neighborhood exactly one city.
data.Description: Promotes city, neighborhood, and address boundaries to first-class rows instead of opaque labels.

data.Applicability: Finite symmetric instance with explicit canonical edge rows.
data.Description: Rejects self-loops and cross-instance edges and checks the expected n(n-1)/2 edge count.

data.Applicability: Candidate supplies one ordered visit list and one edge-bound leg per required stop.
data.Description: Derives supplied-tour validity from visit uniqueness, sequence uniqueness, depot placement, edge membership, global one-in/one-out coverage, unique leg order, and route closure.

data.Applicability: A stop has exactly two remaining admissible incident edges.
data.Description: Next closure target: force both remaining edges at a degree-two stop without branching.

data.Applicability: Neighborhood clusters expose a finite set of admissible boundary crossings.
data.Description: Future city-scale kernel for contracting internally settled neighborhoods into reusable boundary states.

data.Applicability: Every unresolved semantic edge is assigned one obligation kind and one explicit closure criterion.
data.Description: Distinguishes external imports from internal inference, certificate, substrate, generalization, and residual-search obligations.

data.Applicability: Candidate has unique ordered visits and edge-bound leg rows.
data.Description: Requires every ordered visit to have exactly one incoming and one outgoing valid transition and every leg order to be unique.

data.Applicability: Finite symmetric weighted graph with two witnessed cheapest admissible incident edges at every required stop.
data.Description: Sums each stop's two cheapest incident costs, then divides by two because every tour edge contributes at both endpoints.

data.Applicability: Candidate is Hamiltonian, lower bound is certified for the same instance, and candidate cost equals the bound.
data.Description: Closes optimality for one declared finite instance without enumerating all route classes.

data.DisplayName: Route reconstruction from inferred edge set
data.Applicability: Inference has selected a connected degree-two edge set with no proper subtour.
data.Description: Future rule: order a structurally derived cycle rather than consuming a supplied candidate route.

data.Applicability: Run the generated Postgres projection, recreate erb_traveling_salesman, compare vw_* results with Python, and record artifact hashes; otherwise record the exact blocking substrate condition.
data.Description: Attempt generated build, database initialization, and peer conformance; blocked attempts are evidence.

data.Applicability: Represent local-bound applications, antecedents, selected edge states, and support rows without upgrading selected edges to forced edges.
data.Description: Make applications, antecedents, states, decisions, and supports first-class.

data.Applicability: Derive exactly five unique Gridville edges from LocalDegreeBounds and support rows, without using CandidateTour, TourStop, or TourLeg as antecedents.
data.Description: Deduplicate supported local edge selections into a derived edge set.

data.Applicability: Witness selected degree two at all five stops, one connected component, a four-edge spanning tree, and zero proper subtours.
data.Description: Certify degree two, connectedness, spanning-tree coverage, and absence of proper subtours.

data.Applicability: Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
data.Description: Derive route-class and branch metrics from structural closure.

data.Applicability: Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
data.Description: Require sound lower bounds to remain distinct from tightness, connectedness, reconstruction, and optimality.

data.Applicability: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
data.Description: Forbid edges by degree saturation or proper-subtour prevention.

data.Applicability: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
data.Description: Forbid an edge when adding it would close a cycle excluding required stops.

data.Applicability: A recurring surface vocabulary admits explicit definitions over a smaller basis with replayable source mappings.
data.Description: Invent predicates by composing witnessed DAG structure, then retain old names as projections rather than primitives.

data.Applicability: A selected structure has more than one connected component and a Hamiltonian cycle must connect both sides of a nontrivial cut.
data.Description: Any cycle crosses a cut an even number of times; connected coverage makes the count positive, hence at least two.

data.Applicability: A certified local two-factor is disconnected, cut parity supplies mandatory crossings, and insertion/release costs are witnessed.
data.Description: Strengthen a local degree bound by the cheapest witnessed cost of repairing disconnected components into one cycle.

data.Applicability: A supplied, reconstructed, or contracted witness exposes explicit coverage, edge count, cost, defects, and provenance.
data.Description: Treat cycles and paths as one witnessed shape; supplied versus reconstructed becomes provenance rather than ontology.

data.Applicability: Multiple raw witnesses preserve the same boundary signature, coverage, cost, and expansion semantics.
data.Description: Collapse raw witnesses into equivalence classes while retaining expansion certificates.

data.Applicability: An inference step has a rule, input state, output state, antecedent count, decision count, and certificate.
data.Description: Collapse applications, rounds, supports, antecedents, and decisions into one event vocabulary.

data.Applicability: Registered concepts admit explicit definitions over fewer relational atoms and named operators.
data.Description: Separate relational atoms from closure operators while preserving every historical concept as a replayable definition.

data.Applicability: A recurring derived relation is produced by a reusable transformation over typed atoms.
data.Description: Promote closure, aggregation, quotient, and fixed-point transformations to reusable operators rather than primitive predicates.

data.Applicability: Internal path witnesses expose a scope, two ports, full coverage, edge members, order, and cost.
data.Description: Group internal path witnesses that share the same typed boundary ports and coverage scope.

data.Applicability: Two or more valid path witnesses share a boundary fiber and differ only in internal order and valuation.
data.Description: Retain minimum-valued representatives within each boundary fiber and preserve dominated witnesses as expandable provenance.

TSPFrontierObligations.Description: Typed ledger of open and closed semantic frontier obligations. Imported dependencies are used only when an external provider conclusion is actually consumed.

schema.name: ObligationKind
schema.Description: Typed class: imported dependency, inference, certificate, substrate, generalization, or residual search.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: OPEN, CLOSED, BLOCKED, DEFERRED, or FALSIFIED.

schema.name: InferenceRule
schema.Description: Inference contract primarily responsible for closure.

schema.name: OpenedByLoop
schema.Description: Loop that exposed this frontier obligation.

schema.name: ClosedByLoop
schema.Description: Loop that closed the obligation, when closed.

schema.name: TrustDisposition
schema.Description: How the obligation is expected to close or where trust remains.

schema.name: ClosureCriterion
schema.Description: Concrete, auditable condition required for closure.

schema.name: CertificateType
schema.Description: Certificate expected or emitted by closure.

schema.name: IsImportedDependency
schema.Description: Whether this row consumes an external provider conclusion.

schema.name: IsClosed
schema.Description: Whether the frontier obligation is closed.

data.ClosureCriterion: Every visit has exactly one incoming and one outgoing valid transition; adversarial duplicated-transition witness is rejected.
Expected edge count, pair-key correctness, pair uniqueness, endpoint order, and negative duplicate-pair fixture all agree.
effortless build, database recreation, and all vw_* versus Python conformance checks pass with recorded artifact hashes.
Two cheapest incident edges are witnessed at every required stop and half their summed cost is emitted as a certified instance lower bound.
A valid candidate cost equals a certified lower bound for the same finite instance.

data.DisplayName: Route reconstruction from forced structural edges
data.ClosureCriterion: Derive the cycle edge set from inference rows, prove one connected component with no proper subtour, then project ordered tour rows.
Generalize boundary-state coverage, dominance, and expansion soundness beyond three-stop symmetric clusters.
All represented deterministic inference reaches closure; any remaining route classes are explicitly branched with replayable branch certificates.
Represent local-bound applications, antecedents, selected edge states, and support rows without upgrading selected edges to forced edges.
Derive exactly five unique Gridville edges from LocalDegreeBounds and support rows, without using CandidateTour, TourStop, or TourLeg as antecedents.
Witness selected degree two at all five stops, one connected component, a four-edge spanning tree, and zero proper subtours.
Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
On a sparse five-stop fixture, stops with exactly two available incident edges force the five cycle edges with zero branching.
Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.

For each three-stop twin-triangle neighborhood, derive three undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states, reduce six internal orders to three states, and reduce 36 paired internal orders to nine paired boundary-state combinations.
Every baseline surface predicate has a finite expression over exactly eight canonical primitives.
Base six plus mandatory crossing insertion twenty minus internal release two equals certified lower bound twenty-four.
A valid cost-twenty-four cycle equals the certified component-repair lower bound twenty-four.
Supplied and reconstructed valid cycles project to one semantic shape while retaining distinct provenance.
Both twin components are represented as boundary-degree-two quotient nodes with expansion witnesses.
Representative inference and constraint steps project to one event schema without losing provenance.
The basis remains eight while fourteen useful derived terms are coined and all baseline surface predicates retain explicit definitions.

TSPLoops.Description: Named semantic loops that establish the initial Traveling Salesman architecture.

schema.name: PrimaryInferenceRule
schema.Description: Primary inference contract advanced by the loop.

schema.name: NewConcept
schema.Description: Semantic structure added by the loop.

schema.name: WitnessSummary
schema.Description: Concrete evidence produced by the loop.

schema.name: NextFrontier
schema.Description: Unclosed edge exposed by the loop.

schema.name: PlannedClosureCriterion
schema.Description: Auditable condition declared before loop execution.

schema.name: BeforeState
schema.Description: Frontier state recorded before loop execution.

data.WitnessSummary: One city, three neighborhoods, and five addresses form an acyclic spatial hierarchy.
data.NextFrontier: Select addresses into a finite optimization instance.

data.NewConcept: TSP instance, instance stops, and canonical undirected edges
data.WitnessSummary: Five required stops and ten canonical edges satisfy n(n-1)/2 for a complete symmetric graph.
data.NextFrontier: Represent a route as an auditable ordered witness.

data.NewConcept: Candidate tour, ordered stops, and edge-bound legs
data.WitnessSummary: The route A-B-C-D-E-A is represented entirely by rows and relationships.
data.NextFrontier: Derive route validity and reject a structurally deceptive counterexample.

data.NewConcept: Derived Hamiltonian-cycle validity with explicit optimality boundary
data.WitnessSummary: The reference route validates; A-B-C-B-E-A is rejected for duplicate visitation; both retain IsOptimalityProved=false.
data.NextFrontier: Measure how much search remains before adding inference closure.

data.WitnessSummary: For five stops with depot and reversal symmetry fixed, twelve route classes remain and zero have yet been eliminated.
data.NextFrontier: Implement degree-two forced-edge closure and compare BranchCountAfter against twelve.

data.NewConcept: Typed frontier obligations distinct from imported dependencies and residual search
data.WitnessSummary: Eight current frontier rows classify internal inference, certificates, substrate commissioning, generalization, and residual search; active imported dependencies remain zero.
data.NextFrontier: Harden supplied-cycle validity from local leg checks to global transition coverage.

data.NewConcept: Exactly one incoming and outgoing transition per ordered visit
data.WitnessSummary: A candidate with five unique visits and five locally legal legs is rejected because A-B is duplicated and E-A is missing.
data.NextFrontier: Prove graph completeness by unordered pair identity, not edge count alone.

data.NewConcept: Canonical pair key, pair multiplicity, and duplicate-pair negative graph
data.WitnessSummary: A three-stop graph with the expected three rows is rejected because AB occurs twice and BC is absent.
data.NextFrontier: Construct a reusable lower bound from the Hamiltonian degree-two requirement.

data.NewConcept: Local two-cheapest incident-edge witnesses and global double-counting bound
data.WitnessSummary: Local bounds 5,5,6,6,6 sum to 28; dividing by two certifies every Gridville tour costs at least 14.
data.NextFrontier: Compare the certified lower bound with a valid candidate upper bound.

data.WitnessSummary: The valid reference tour costs 14 and the certified lower bound is 14, so it is optimal for tsp-gridville-5 without route enumeration.
data.NextFrontier: Reconstruct the cycle from inferred structural edges rather than a supplied route.

data.WitnessSummary: Generated Postgres and Python agree on all declared semantics; Decimal-normalized live checks and append-only attempt history close the substrate obligation.
data.NextFrontier: Create a first-class inference application spine independent of substrate availability.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Run the generated Postgres projection, recreate erb_traveling_salesman, compare vw_* results with Python, and record artifact hashes; otherwise record the exact blocking substrate condition.
data.BeforeState: frontier-postgres-commissioning is OPEN; no execution-run certificate exists
data.AfterState: Attempts 1-9 preserve missing-CLI, npm, workflow, stale-oracle, chained-finalizer, and numeric-presentation failures. Retry 10 installed the pinned CLI archive, generated the complete vw_* projection, initialized erb_traveling_salesman, passed rulebook-derived Python/Postgres conformance, normalized NUMERIC text through Decimal, and durably sealed the substrate certificate.

data.WitnessSummary: Represented 5 applications, 5 antecedents, 5 selected edge states, and 10 supports; no edge was labeled FORCED.
data.NextFrontier: Union supported edge selections into a derived structural edge set.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent local-bound applications, antecedents, selected edge states, and support rows without upgrading selected edges to forced edges.
data.BeforeState: Inference rules exist, but individual applications, antecedents, edge decisions, and supports are not first-class rows.
data.AfterState: Represented 5 applications, 5 antecedents, 5 selected edge states, and 10 supports; no edge was labeled FORCED.

data.WitnessSummary: Derived 5 unique edges ['edge-a-b', 'edge-a-e', 'edge-b-c', 'edge-c-d', 'edge-d-e'] at total cost 14; no CandidateTour/TourStop/TourLeg row was an antecedent.
data.NextFrontier: Prove the derived edge set is connected and degree two at every required stop.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Derive exactly five unique Gridville edges from LocalDegreeBounds and support rows, without using CandidateTour, TourStop, or TourLeg as antecedents.
data.BeforeState: Local degree bounds identify edges independently at stops, but their deduplicated union is not a first-class object.
data.AfterState: Derived 5 unique edges ['edge-a-b', 'edge-a-e', 'edge-b-c', 'edge-c-d', 'edge-d-e'] at total cost 14; no CandidateTour/TourStop/TourLeg row was an antecedent.

data.WitnessSummary: All five stops have selected degree two; component count=1, spanning-tree edges=4, proper subtours=0.
data.NextFrontier: Reconstruct an ordered cycle from the certified edge set.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Witness selected degree two at all five stops, one connected component, a four-edge spanning tree, and zero proper subtours.
data.BeforeState: The inferred Gridville edge union has no represented degree, component, spanning-tree, or subtour certificate.
data.AfterState: All five stops have selected degree two; component count=1, spanning-tree edges=4, proper subtours=0.

data.WitnessSummary: Reconstructed stop-a -> stop-b -> stop-c -> stop-d -> stop-e -> stop-a at cost 14; CandidateUsedAsAntecedent=false and the supplied route is comparison-only.
data.NextFrontier: Derive route-class and branch-elimination metrics from the reconstructed result.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Starting at the depot with deterministic orientation, project five ordered reconstruction steps from the inferred edge set with CandidateUsedAsAntecedent=false.
data.BeforeState: Gridville has a certified cycle edge set, but the only ordered route rows are supplied candidates.
data.AfterState: Reconstructed stop-a -> stop-b -> stop-c -> stop-d -> stop-e -> stop-a at cost 14; CandidateUsedAsAntecedent=false and the supplied route is comparison-only.

data.WitnessSummary: Derived 12 initial route classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity.
data.NextFrontier: Add a fixture where the degree-two lower bound is sound but non-tight.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Derive 12 initial symmetry-reduced classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity for Gridville.
data.BeforeState: Route-discovery counts are recorded as baseline data and are not tied to a structural reconstruction certificate.
data.AfterState: Derived 12 initial route classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity.

data.WitnessSummary: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.
data.NextFrontier: Implement generic degree-two forcing on a sparse availability fixture.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
data.BeforeState: The only positive fixture has a tight degree-two lower bound, so failure behavior is not represented.
data.AfterState: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.

data.WitnessSummary: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.
data.NextFrontier: Propagate degree saturation and proper-subtour forbidden-edge consequences.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: On a sparse five-stop fixture, stops with exactly two available incident edges force the five cycle edges with zero branching.
data.BeforeState: Degree-two forcing is declared but has no executable state, round, or decision certificates.
data.AfterState: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.

data.WitnessSummary: Forbade sparse B-D by degree saturation and twin WA-WC by proper-subtour prevention; both decisions are deterministic and branch-free.
data.NextFrontier: Contract neighborhood internals into finite boundary states.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
data.BeforeState: Forced edges do not yet emit deterministic forbidden-edge consequences or premature-subtour exclusions.
data.AfterState: Forbade sparse B-D by degree saturation and twin WA-WC by proper-subtour prevention; both decisions are deterministic and branch-free.

data.WitnessSummary: Each twin-triangle neighborhood contracts six raw internal orders to three undirected boundary states (50%); paired composition contracts 36 combinations to nine (75%) with cost-preserving path witnesses.
data.NextFrontier: Generalize boundary-state soundness across non-triangular clusters and then branch only on residual city-level ambiguity.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: For each three-stop twin-triangle neighborhood, derive three undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states, reduce six internal orders to three states, and reduce 36 paired internal orders to nine paired boundary-state combinations.
data.BeforeState: Neighborhood rows are geographic labels only; no executable entry/exit path states or contraction certificate exists.
data.AfterState: Each twin-triangle neighborhood contracts six raw internal orders to three undirected boundary states (50%); paired composition contracts 36 combinations to nine (75%) with cost-preserving path witnesses.

data.WitnessSummary: The convergence hypothesis is now versioned data, explicitly exploratory and not a theorem.
data.NextFrontier: Derive the explicit predicate basis and map the surface vocabulary onto it.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Record the working prediction, baseline thirty-two surface predicates, target eight-predicate basis, and DAG-based predicate-invention method without claiming a theorem.
data.BeforeState: Forty physical tables expose at least thirty-two recurring surface predicates, but no explicit convergence hypothesis or semantic compression baseline is recorded.
data.AfterState: Recorded a thirty-two-surface-predicate baseline, an eight-predicate target, and the prediction that DAG inference will invent derived predicates while the primitive basis stabilizes.

data.WitnessSummary: Predicate basis = {MEMBERSHIP, INCIDENCE, CARDINALITY, ORDER, WEIGHT, COMMITMENT, CONNECTIVITY, PROVENANCE}; measured semantic compression is 75%.
data.NextFrontier: Collapse edge-decision vocabulary into one ordered commitment concept.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent exactly eight canonical primitives and map all thirty-two baseline surface predicates to finite basis expressions.
data.BeforeState: The convergence prediction is recorded, but the candidate primitive vocabulary and surface-to-basis map do not exist.
data.AfterState: Mapped thirty-two recurring surface predicates to eight canonical primitives; historical names remain queryable projections rather than independent primitives.

data.WitnessSummary: Gridville selections have rank 1 and CURRENT_WITNESS scope; sparse forced edges have rank 2 and ALL_FEASIBLE_TOURS scope; no epistemic upgrade was hidden.
data.NextFrontier: Collapse degree checks, local bounds, and forcing into one incidence budget.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project all edge statuses onto one commitment rank, polarity, necessity scope, and provenance representation without conflating selected with forced.
data.BeforeState: UNKNOWN, SELECTED, FORCED, FORBIDDEN, CONTRADICTED, and SUPERSEDED are separate surface statuses split across edge-state and constraint-decision projections.
data.AfterState: Collapsed edge-state and constraint-decision vocabulary into a commitment lattice with rank, polarity, necessity scope, and provenance.

data.WitnessSummary: All five Gridville and all six twin-triangle stops have required incidence 2, observed incidence 2, and incidence defect 0; connectivity remains a separate coordinate.
data.NextFrontier: Lift local incidence defects into a global multi-coordinate defect vector.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent required incidence, observed incidence, and incidence defect uniformly for every Gridville and twin-triangle stop.
data.BeforeState: Cycle degree, selected degree, remaining degree, local lower-bound incidence, and forcing applicability are represented as separate concepts.
data.AfterState: Projected local degree bounds, cycle degree, selected degree, and forcing applicability onto one incidence budget.

data.WitnessSummary: Gridville vector = 0|0|0|0; twin local two-factor vector = 0|1|2|18, locating the exact unresolved geometry without inventing a new primitive.
data.NextFrontier: Derive the minimum crossing demand exposed by the twin component defect.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent incidence, connectivity, boundary, and cost defects as one vector and distinguish zero-defect Gridville from twin triangles.
data.BeforeState: Degree violations, connected-component count, boundary crossings, and cost gaps are separate acceptance surfaces.
data.AfterState: Collapsed incidence violations, component excess, missing boundary crossings, and bound gap into one defect vector.

data.WitnessSummary: Twin component cut: crossing count is even for every cycle and nonzero for connected coverage; therefore crossing count >= 2.
data.NextFrontier: Turn mandatory crossings into an additive component-repair lower bound.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Derive that a Hamiltonian cycle crosses a nontrivial component cut a positive even number of times, hence at least twice.
data.BeforeState: Twin triangles exposes two selected components, but the mandatory number of crossings between them is not represented.
data.AfterState: Derived a positive-even cut-crossing certificate for the two twin components, with minimum crossing demand two.

data.WitnessSummary: Twin component repair adjustment is +18; the strengthened finite-instance lower bound is 24.
data.NextFrontier: Compare the repaired lower bound with the feasible candidate upper bound.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Add two cost-ten crossings, release two cost-one internal edges, and certify an eighteen-unit repair adjustment and lower bound twenty-four.
data.BeforeState: Twin triangles has a certified local degree bound of six and a two-crossing requirement, but no composed global repair cost.
data.AfterState: Composed the local degree bound with cut repair: 6 + (2 x 10) - (2 x 1) = 24.

data.WitnessSummary: The original degree-only bound remains recorded as non-tight; the composed component-repair bound equals the valid candidate and proves optimality only for tsp-twin-triangles-6.
data.NextFrontier: Normalize supplied and reconstructed routes into one witness shape.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Close twin-triangle finite optimality by lower-bound/upper-bound equality while retaining the original non-tight local-bound counterexample.
data.BeforeState: Twin triangles has a repaired lower bound of twenty-four and a feasible candidate of cost twenty-four, but no finite optimality certificate.
data.AfterState: Closed twin-triangle optimality by the sandwich 24 <= candidate cost 24 <= 24.

data.WitnessSummary: The supplied and reconstructed Gridville cycles share coverage, edge count, cost, and zero-defect semantic key; only provenance differs.
data.NextFrontier: Extend the same normal form to neighborhood entry/exit paths.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project supplied and reconstructed cycles into one normal form whose semantic key differs only by provenance where the underlying cycle is identical.
data.BeforeState: Candidate tours, reconstructed tours, and cluster paths use separate object vocabularies despite sharing coverage, incidence, order, connectivity, cost, and provenance.
data.AfterState: Normalized supplied and reconstructed cycles into one semantic witness shape.

data.WitnessSummary: Each path normal form records scope, canonical entry/exit, three-stop coverage, two edges, cost two, zero defects, and expansion provenance.
data.NextFrontier: Quotient boundary witnesses by semantic equivalence rather than raw orientation.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project all six cluster boundary states into valid path normal forms keyed by scope, entry, exit, coverage, and cost.
data.BeforeState: Neighborhood boundary states are special-purpose rows rather than path witnesses in the common normal form.
data.AfterState: Projected all six neighborhood boundary states into valid path witness normal forms.

data.WitnessSummary: Each neighborhood has six directed orders / orientation multiplicity two = three quotient classes; the product quotient has 3 x 3 = 9 classes.
data.NextFrontier: Collapse neighborhoods and disconnected components into one boundary object.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent reversal-equivalent internal orders as quotient classes and derive three classes per neighborhood and nine product classes.
data.BeforeState: The measured six-to-three neighborhood contraction is stored as a count reduction without an explicit equivalence relation.
data.AfterState: Recast neighborhood contraction as a semantic quotient by path reversal with fixed unordered boundary, coverage, and cost.

data.WitnessSummary: The city-level object is now a two-node quotient graph; cluster versus component is provenance, while boundary degree and expansion signature carry the semantics.
data.NextFrontier: Collapse applications, rounds, supports, antecedents, and decisions into closure events.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent the two twin neighborhoods as quotient nodes with boundary degree two, unifying cluster and component interfaces.
data.BeforeState: Neighborhood clusters and selected connected components are treated as different kinds of objects despite exposing the same boundary-degree contract.
data.AfterState: Unified the two neighborhoods and the two selected connected components as quotient nodes with required boundary degree two.

data.WitnessSummary: 14 closure events cover selection, union, rigidity, reconstruction, forcing, forbidding, cut parity, repair, optimality, and quotienting.
data.NextFrontier: Measure whether new derived vocabulary has stopped increasing the primitive basis.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Project representative selection, union, rigidity, reconstruction, forcing, forbidding, cut, repair, optimality, and quotient steps into one closure-event schema.
data.BeforeState: Inference applications, antecedents, supports, states, constraint rounds, and decisions are separate execution concepts.
data.AfterState: Collapsed applications, states, antecedents, supports, rounds, and decisions into a common closure-event projection.

data.WitnessSummary: Convergence event: semantic compression 75%, physical tables 40 to 45, coined terms 14, new primitives after basis discovery 0; status is early empirical support for this finite TSP domain.
data.NextFrontier: Stress the eight-predicate basis on larger clusters and branch only after closure leaves a witnessed residual kernel.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Certify thirty-two-to-eight semantic compression, fourteen coined terms, zero new primitives after the basis loop, bounded physical schema growth, and an explicit residual kernel.
data.BeforeState: Thirteen reduction loops have coined derived predicates, but their cumulative compression and primitive-basis stability are not certified.
data.AfterState: Certified a stable eight-predicate basis across fourteen reduction loops: thirty-two baseline surface predicates compress to eight primitives, fourteen useful terms were coined, and no new primitive appeared after loop 598.

data.WitnessSummary: All eight loop-610 primitives retain their historical identity and receive explicit semantic-category and reduced-expression fields; current primitive count remains eight. The convergence objective is coherence rather than monotone row, table, or predicate-count reduction.
data.NextFrontier: Test whether membership and incidence share one typed attachment atom.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Classify the eight historical predicates as candidate atoms or operator-derived concepts, preserve their loop-610 provenance, and record that local expansion is permitted when an opaque dependency is replaced by explicit reusable machinery. Measure convergence by coherence, reuse, trust-boundary reduction, and residual ambiguity rather than by monotone physical size.
data.BeforeState: Loop 610 records eight canonical predicates, but relational atoms and reusable operators are not distinguished. Convergence could also be misread as requiring monotone table or predicate shrinkage.
data.AfterState: Separated the historical eight-predicate basis into candidate relational atoms and operator-derived concepts without changing the active basis. Recorded that local representational growth is compatible with global semantic convergence when it replaces opacity with reusable structure.

data.WitnessSummary: Every historical membership/incidence surface retains a reduced expression, while the active primitive count becomes seven.
data.NextFrontier: Test whether cardinality, order, and weight share one valuation atom.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Introduce ATTACHMENT(subject, object, role), reduce MEMBERSHIP and INCIDENCE to typed attachment roles, and preserve every historical surface definition.
data.BeforeState: Membership and incidence are separate primitives even though both attach one object to a typed scope or endpoint.
data.AfterState: Collapsed membership and incidence into ATTACHMENT with typed roles such as member, endpoint, entry, exit, and uses.

data.WitnessSummary: Degree is a valuation under incident-count, sequence is a valuation under ordinal-position, and cost is a valuation under the objective measure; active primitive count becomes five.
data.NextFrontier: Test whether commitment and provenance share one warrant atom.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Introduce VALUATION(subject, measure, value), reduce count, ordinal, and cost predicates to valuation forms, and retain aggregation as an explicit pending operator.
data.BeforeState: Cardinality, order, and weight remain separate primitives despite all assigning values under a named measure.
data.AfterState: Collapsed cardinality, order, and weight into VALUATION under explicit measures.

data.WitnessSummary: SELECTED remains a current-witness warrant, FORCED remains an all-feasible-tours warrant, and historical provenance remains queryable; active primitive count becomes four.
data.NextFrontier: Move connectivity from the atom basis into a closure operator.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Introduce WARRANT(conclusion, source, modality), reduce commitment and provenance to warrant projections, and preserve necessity scope distinctions.
data.BeforeState: Commitment and provenance are separate primitives even though every commitment is a scoped conclusion warranted by sources and rules.
data.AfterState: Collapsed commitment and provenance into WARRANT with explicit source, modality, and scope.

data.WitnessSummary: The current active atom count is three: ATTACHMENT, VALUATION, WARRANT. Connectedness, components, cuts, and reachability are closure results.
data.NextFrontier: Lift count, sum, minimum, and parity into one aggregation operator.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Register CLOSURE as an operator over attachment relations, reduce CONNECTIVITY to closure, and leave exactly three active relational atoms.
data.BeforeState: Connectivity is still treated as a primitive rather than reachability closure over typed attachments.
data.AfterState: Lifted connectivity from the primitive basis into CLOSURE over typed attachments.

data.WitnessSummary: Degree, edge count, lower-bound sum, fiber minimum, and convergence measurement now differ by measure and reducer, not by primitive type.
data.NextFrontier: Lift symmetry and dominance contraction into one quotient operator.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Register AGGREGATE as the common count/sum/min/max/parity operator over attachments and valuations, with no new atom.
data.BeforeState: Counts, sums, minima, degrees, and repair totals are expressed through many surface formulas without one operator identity.
data.AfterState: Lifted count, sum, minimum, maximum, parity, and signed repair arithmetic into AGGREGATE.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Lift repeated closure rounds into one fixed-point operator.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Register QUOTIENT as the operator that identifies witnesses under an explicit equivalence relation and retains representatives plus expansion provenance.
data.BeforeState: Semantic Quotient and Component Quotient are coined predicates but quotienting is not represented as a reusable operator.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Certify the current three-atom/four-operator factorization.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Register FIXPOINT as repeated warranted closure until no commitment changes, and map inference rounds and closure events onto it.
data.BeforeState: Closure events and constraint rounds expose repeated application, but fixed-point iteration is not explicit in the semantic basis.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Stress the reduced basis on a general boundary-port interface.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Certify ATTACHMENT, VALUATION, and WARRANT as the current atoms; CLOSURE, AGGREGATE, QUOTIENT, and FIXPOINT as operators; and express every registered concept without the old eight primitive tokens.
data.BeforeState: Three active atoms and four operators have emerged incrementally, but their coverage of the historical vocabulary is not certified.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Build a non-symmetric four-stop neighborhood and group its path witnesses by boundary fiber.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent every existing cluster path through typed entry/exit ports, a common port contract, orientation multiplicity, and a boundary-fiber key.
data.BeforeState: Entry and exit stops remain special cluster fields rather than typed attachments with a reusable port contract.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Collapse each boundary fiber by valuation dominance.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Represent an asymmetric four-stop complete neighborhood with twelve reversal-quotient path states grouped into six boundary fibers, retaining twenty-four directed orientations and explicit expansion members.
data.BeforeState: The three-stop fixture has one path per unordered boundary, so it cannot distinguish quotienting by symmetry from competing internal valuations.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Certify the combined reversal and valuation quotient.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Within each of six boundary fibers, warrant the unique minimum-cost path, mark the other path dominated, and record the exact dominance delta.
data.BeforeState: Each asymmetric boundary fiber contains two non-equivalent internal path valuations and no canonical survivor.

data.WitnessSummary: Planned predicate-refinement loop; no closure claim yet.
data.NextFrontier: Generalize region repair across more than two quotient components, then expose a genuine residual branch orbit.
data.PlannedClosureCriterion: Certify 24 directed paths to 12 reversal classes to 6 boundary-fiber minima, with expansion, coverage, and cost preservation, while the physical table count remains unchanged.
data.BeforeState: The original contraction certificate only demonstrates three-stop reversal symmetry and does not test the reduced basis against asymmetric valuations.

TSPConvergenceMeasurements.Description: Measurements testing whether surface concepts compress onto a stable predicate basis.

schema.name: MeasurementKind
schema.Description: Baseline, reduction, stability, or final convergence measurement.

schema.name: NewPrimitiveCount
schema.Description: New primitives introduced by this loop.

schema.name: DerivedAliasCount
schema.Description: Named derived concepts retained above the basis.

schema.name: PhysicalTableCount
schema.Description: Canonical physical table count at this loop.

schema.name: NovelTerm
schema.Description: Predicate coined or stabilized by the loop.

data.Notes: Working prediction only: concepts will begin to collapse, useful terms will continue to be coined, and convergence will be measured rather than assumed.
All thirty-two baseline surface predicates have explicit definitions over MEMBERSHIP, INCIDENCE, CARDINALITY, ORDER, WEIGHT, COMMITMENT, CONNECTIVITY, and PROVENANCE.
Six edge statuses now project through one commitment rank/polarity/scope concept; selected remains weaker than forced.
Eleven stop-level degree surfaces share required incidence, observed incidence, and one defect formula.
Four previously separate acceptance surfaces become coordinates of one defect vector.
The crossing obligation is a composition of connectivity, incidence, and cardinality, not a ninth primitive.
Local degree and global component repair compose additively without extending the predicate basis.
Lower bound, feasible upper witness, and equality rigidity collapse into one interval certificate.
Supplied and reconstructed Gridville cycles have the same semantic key; origin is provenance only.
Cycle and path witnesses now share one normal form; boundary-specific meaning is a signature, not an object type.
The previous count reduction now has an explicit equivalence relation and representative contract.
Geographic clusters and selected graph components now expose the same quotient-node boundary contract.
Six execution projections now share one event schema with explicit input, output, rule, antecedent count, decision count, and certificate.
Fourteen named predicates were coined, but no primitive was added after loop 598. Five generic tables preserve replay while semantic compression remains 75%.

The loop-610 basis is preserved as history while candidates for atoms and operators are explicitly classified. Local representation may grow when an opaque node is decomposed; support is measured by increasing semantic reuse, explicit warrants, smaller opaque trust boundaries, and reduced residual ambiguity.
Membership and incidence are role-specialized attachments; active primitive count falls from eight to seven.
Counts, ordinals, costs, bounds, ranks, and deltas become named valuations; active primitive count falls to five.
Commitment and provenance become one scoped warrant relation without erasing selected-versus-forced modality; active primitives fall to four.
Connectivity becomes reachability closure over attachments; active relational primitives are ATTACHMENT, VALUATION, and WARRANT.
Counts, sums, minima, maxima, parity, degree, and repair totals share one operator over attachments and valuations.

TSPConceptRegistry.Description: Registry of primitive, derived, coined, and historical concepts with explicit basis expressions.

schema.name: BasisExpression
schema.Description: Definition over the eight primitive predicates.

schema.name: Arity
schema.Description: Declared argument count for the concept.

schema.name: IntroducedByLoop
schema.Description: Loop introducing or registering the concept.

schema.name: SupersededByConcept
schema.Description: Canonical coined concept replacing this surface name, when applicable.

schema.name: HistoricalConceptKind
schema.Description: Concept kind at the time of its historical certificate.

schema.name: ReducedBasisExpression
schema.Description: Current definition over active atoms and operators.

schema.name: OperatorExpression
schema.Description: Operators required to derive the concept from atoms.

schema.name: ReductionGeneration
schema.Description: Reduction generation introducing the current definition.

schema.name: IsCurrentBasisMember
schema.Description: Whether the row is an active atom or operator.

SearchMetrics.Description: Quantitative record of how much combinatorial search remains after each represented inference stage.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the metric row.

schema.name: SearchQuestion
schema.Description: Exact question whose remaining branch count is being measured.

schema.name: CandidateTour
schema.Description: Candidate involved when measuring verification rather than route discovery.

schema.name: SymmetryClassCount
schema.Description: Route classes after fixing depot and reversal symmetry.

schema.name: BranchCountBefore
schema.Description: Branches before the current inference stage.

schema.name: BranchCountAfter
schema.Description: Branches after the current inference stage.

schema.name: ResidualAmbiguityCount
schema.Description: Remaining unresolved route classes or boundary states.

schema.name: SearchEliminationPct
schema.Description: Percentage of branches removed without search.

TSPInvariantChecks.Description: Rulebook-native acceptance checks comparing expected raw outcomes to derived candidate-tour fields.

schema.name: ActualHamiltonianCycleWitness
schema.Description: Derived route-validity result from the candidate-tour view.

schema.name: ActualOptimalityProved
schema.Description: Derived optimality-certificate status from the candidate-tour view.

schema.name: ExpectedTotalTravelCost
schema.Description: Expected sum of represented valid leg costs.

schema.name: ActualTotalTravelCost
schema.Description: Derived total tour cost from the candidate-tour view.

schema.name: IsHamiltonianStatusCorrect
schema.Description: Whether expected and actual route validity agree.

schema.name: IsOptimalityStatusCorrect
schema.Description: Whether expected and actual optimality status agree.

schema.name: IsCostCorrect
schema.Description: Whether expected and actual total cost agree.

schema.name: IsPassing
schema.Description: Whether all represented expectations agree with derived values.

data.DisplayName: Reference route validates and matches the certified finite-instance lower bound
Locally legal legs cannot hide duplicated and missing global transitions
Twin candidate matches the component-repair lower bound
